mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors price in future rate cuts.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2% for three consecutive months, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates labor market overheating, prompting Fed rate hikes that drive S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) or 20-day returns exceed 1.5% despite the labor market signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a demand-side slowdown, causing S&P 500 declines during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The interaction between HOUST and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns (R-squared < 0.05) or 20-day SPX returns exceed 2% during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as firms benefit from higher capacity utilization.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) or 5-day SPX returns remain negative despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) signals weak consumer demand, leading to S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days as earnings expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when RSAFS falls below 350,000 for three consecutive months, or the correlation between RSAFS and SPX returns exceeds -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal aggressive monetary tightening, causing S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (p > 0.10) or 20-day SPX returns exceed 1.5% during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 5%) combined with stagnant payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a weak labor market, driving S&P 500 declines during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: The interaction between UNRATE and PAYEMS fails to predict SPX returns (R-squared < 0.05) or 60-day SPX returns exceed 3% during high-unemployment periods.
